comparisons.evidence: Layer3 has resting/sweep proxies but no reclaim, acceptance, or rank; ZoneEngine exposes none of those liquidity semantics.
Simple liquidity uses internal clustering/same-candle scoring, while layer3 emits prior-touch counts and next-candle-confirmed sweep records.
The files are byte-identical and independent freezes produce identical cluster events and the same committed-config failure.
Neither surface exposes the required lifecycle and destination-ranking contract; ZoneEngine is FVG/order-block focused.
The byte-duplicate simple surface and layer3 expose different outputs, parameters, and confirmation horizons.
The compared sources do not expose compatible lifecycle or ranking semantics.
The selected confirmed high/low fixture matches after mapping legacy window=2 to layer2 lookback=2 and min_strength=0.0; this is fixture equivalence only.
The vector surface emits extra pivots and accepts a candidate with one right candle; source hashes differ.
No listed structure surface emits a complete or incomplete MSS sequence contract.
Legacy whole-series ATR makes a nominally confirmed pivot depend on later candles; layer2 stays stable at its declared availability.
Vector alignment emits additional pivots and insufficient-right output that symmetric layer2 does not emit.

fixtures.purpose: High-side close-through followed by next-candle bearish reversal.
High-side wick raid with next-candle bearish reversal.
Low-side raid with next-candle bullish reversal.
Three prior equal highs and lows within ATR tolerance.
Multiple unswept candidate levels with no source ranking contract.
Unswept high/low pools and adapter BSL/SSL registration mapping.
Confirmed high/low pivots and duplicate-engine divergence.
Whole-series ATR mean changes a previously confirmable pivot.
Later swing changes simple BOS evaluation at an earlier candle.
Candidate pivot with only one right-side candle.
Bearish/bullish BOS and both CHoCH transitions.
A candidate CHoCH/new-swing sequence without confirming BOS.
A candidate CHoCH/new-swing/BOS sequence for missing MSS capability.
Bullish and bearish simple-engine BOS at its configured displacement.

capabilities.evidence: Both directions are emitted; source logic uses high/low wick penetration.
Both trend transitions are emitted by the source state variable.
Symmetric lookback pivots are observed and delayed by the adapter until confirmation.
No MSS sequence output exists despite module prose.
No CHoCH/new-swing/confirming-BOS MSS contract is implemented.

temporal_checks.evidence: AST scan found no bfill/backfill operation.
AST scan found no rolling call with center=True.
Swing pivots require a symmetric right window and are emitted with pivot timestamps only.
AST scan found no negative shift call.
A confirmed pivot is identical on the full fixture and a prefix ending at its declared availability.
AST scan found no direct whole-series min/max call.
BOS/CHoCH compare high and low wicks rather than close.

capabilities.evidence: A mitigation score distinguishes close placement, but it is not an acceptance state or hold-count contract.
RESTING_HIGH and RESTING_LOW are explicitly preserved as adapter-labeled BSL/SSL proxies.
Multiple candidates retain source input order, but no score, rank, tie, or as-of contract exists.
Both prior-touch counters are emitted at the current candle.
No reclaimed lifecycle state or confirmation rule is exposed.
High-side and low-side sweeps require a later reversal candle.
Resting output is frozen as an untouched proxy, not a complete lifecycle state.
The same sweep type carries mitigation_score 0.70 for wick-only and 0.95 for close-through; no acceptance label is emitted.

AST scan found no rolling call with center=True.

Every synthetic sweep references a pool whose supplied confirmation index precedes the raid index.
process derives resting levels after computing the complete-frame sweep list and emits no as-of timestamp.

The private clustering helper deterministically groups price values within the ATR gap.

detect_sweeps cannot run with the committed market configuration (KeyError:liquidity); no configuration is patched by characterization.

limitations: Characterization freezes observed software behavior on synthetic fixtures; it does not prove universal equivalence or market validity.
MSS, reclaimed/accepted lifecycle states, and destination ranking remain BLOCKED_BY_MISSING_SEMANTICS.
Simple structure and liquidity thresholds use complete-frame ATR means and are not real-time eligible.
Layer2 BOS/CHoCH uses wick penetration, which diverges from the governed close-based definition.
Layer3 sweeps require the next candle but are written at the raid row; downstream eligibility begins at confirmation.
Resting liquidity is derived from a complete sweep list without an as-of timestamp and cannot be promoted to a historical lifecycle state.
OHLCV-based BSL/SSL labels are deterministic proxies and do not evidence actual resting orders.
G6-G9 remain NOT_EVALUATED; this artifact does not establish analytical, statistical, or execution readiness.
